input_summary.text: Emergency flood warning. Click this link now to verify your location.

matched_threats.description: The submitted URL appears suspicious or untrusted.
The message uses urgent wording and asks the user to click a link.
The message claims to be an emergency alert but comes from an unknown source.
The source is unknown, which increases the chance of misinformation.

recommended_controls.purpose: Validate submitted URL, text, timestamp, hazard information, and source before processing.
Ensure only authenticated users or trusted systems can access the risk assessment API.
Restrict access to risk assessment features based on user roles and permissions.
Prevent API abuse, spam requests, brute-force attempts, and denial-of-service activity.
Check whether the submitted alert comes from a trusted and verified source.
Verify that disaster alerts have not been forged, modified, or injected by an attacker.
Record risk assessment requests and decisions for monitoring and investigation.
Detect phishing-style language, suspicious wording, and harmful message patterns.

data.explanation: The input was classified as malicious because the AI model returned a high risk score, the message used urgent phishing-style wording, the alert was connected to an emergency disaster situation, and the source was unknown.
